# ECDSA Key Recovery via Weak Nonce Reuse
# Recover the private key from two ECDSA signatures that reuse the same nonce (k).
#
# Curve: NIST256p (secp256r1)
# p = 115792089210356248762697446949407573530086143415290314195533631308867097853951
# a = -3
# b = 41058363725152142129326129780047268409114441015993725554835256314039467401291
# Gx = 48439561293906451759052585252797914202762949526041747995844080717082404635286
# Gy = 36134250956749795798585127919587881956611106672985015071877198253568414405109
# n = 115792089210356248762697446949407573529996955224135760342422259061068512044369
#
# Message 1: Cyber Guardians Society CTF 2026
# z1 (SHA-256 hash) = 73513118436320268766219626951168602612467369058851043135449887315144516969528
# Message 2: ECDSA Nonce Reuse Attack Demo
# z2 (SHA-256 hash) = 40491995092039904533884757896759574219867116224309096604647588526368545344322
#
# Signatures (r, s1) and (r, s2) - same r indicates same nonce k
# r = 113672263116997988963422843336732598436413491673093427510593090874115734366920
# s1 = 85116277649285188111943145636986304316118645241275358362619266247620653184833
# s2 = 54195363221292254809572328868255926702477766622621188551519330080892519067657
#
# Public key Q = (55501476895472809106802017567153574883414578957693224916774134602178664179660,
#                 12622616971740003028805275445288205072627262803771841788824987511177557172414)
#
# Recovery steps:
# 1. k = ((z1 - z2) * pow(s1 - s2, -1, n)) % n
# 2. d = (pow(r, -1, n) * (s1 * k - z1)) % n
# 3. flag = d.to_bytes(32, 'big').lstrip(b'\x00').decode()
#
# Fake flags (appear via strings command):
CGS{ecdsa_key_recovery}
CGS{nonce_reuse}
CGS{key_extraction}
